message: ft-xbnl0.2.4: align verifier target dir and tcp tls bench

@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","title":"Build a leak-risk inventory and instrumentation layer for mux and runtime lifecycles","description":"# Goal
Make leak and retention risk visible before trying to fix it.

This task exists because the project cannot close the original memory-leak complaint by intuition. Pane, tab, window, session, subscription, callback, channel, scope, file-descriptor, and scrollback lifecycles all need instrumentation so future fixes are tied to evidence rather than anecdotes.","design":"# Design
1. Inventory the lifecycle surfaces most likely to retain memory or file descriptors in mux and runtime hot paths.
2. Add counters, gauges, traces, or debug dumps that let those surfaces be observed during churn, reconnect, restore, and long-haul runs.
3. Make the instrumentation cheap enough to use in soak and benchmark harnesses.
4. Feed the resulting metrics into later leak-oracle, soak, and release-gate work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Leak-prone lifecycle surfaces have explicit instrumentation or debug evidence hooks.
2. The instrumentation is documented well enough that future agents can tell what each signal means.
3. Later remediation and soak tasks can rely on this instrumentation instead of inventing their own ad hoc probes.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative capture showing the instrumentation in use.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is the measurement prerequisite for the rest of the reliability lane. Without it, remediation claims will stay fuzzy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.263296Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-04T07:01:39.719408Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T07:01:39.719211Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","leaks","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.619426Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:38.844333Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.112085Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.263296Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2829,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Agent Mail DB calls are still failing for , , and file reservations, so I am using Beads comments as the durable coordination trail while proceeding. Starting implementation on the existing  pipeline: add a serializable leak-risk inventory snapshot in , populate it from  using  +  +  in , and surface it through  plus schema/back-compat tests in . Focus is on counts and gauges already reachable today: tracked/observed panes, tabs, windows, workspaces, pane-arena tracked bytes, cursor snapshot bytes, storage lock contention, and watchdog health/heartbeat telemetry so later , , and  can build on an authoritative substrate instead of inventing new probes.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:06:34Z"},{"id":2830,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Correction to the prior comment: Agent Mail DB calls are still failing for send_message, fetch_inbox, and file reservations, so I am using Beads comments as the durable coordination trail while proceeding. Starting implementation on the existing HealthSnapshot pipeline: add a serializable leak-risk inventory snapshot in crates/frankenterm-core/src/crash.rs, populate it from ObservationRuntime using PaneRegistry + RuntimeMetrics + HeartbeatRegistry in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs, and surface it through HealthSnapshotRenderer plus schema/back-compat tests in crates/frankenterm-core/src/output/renderers.rs. Focus is on counts and gauges already reachable today: tracked and observed panes, tabs, windows, workspaces, pane-arena tracked bytes, cursor snapshot bytes, storage lock contention, and watchdog health and heartbeat telemetry so later ft-xbnl0.4.2, ft-xbnl0.4.3, and ft-xbnl0.5.6 can build on an authoritative substrate instead of inventing new probes.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:06:47Z"},{"id":2836,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Progress update: the fail-closed goal-line harness tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_4_1_leak_risk_inventory.sh is running against the final tree under artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.1/leak_risk_inventory/20260403T212641Z. I switched the harness to explicit RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1 because docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md already allows that path when metadata is recorded, and the initial default smoke run was burning a duplicate full-repo sync before the first material remote step. Current first step is runtime_inventory_unit on worker vmi1153651 with a fail-closed remote cargo test and no fail-open signals so far.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:34:25Z"},{"id":2837,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification blocker found during the first fail-closed harness run: cargo test for frankenterm-core failed remotely in frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs with an ambiguous poll_write call while building dependency crate async_ossl. I applied the minimal blocker fix in that dirty file by changing poll_write_vectored to call <Self as AsyncWrite>::poll_write(self, cx, buf), which preserves the surrounding async-asupersync migration work instead of rewriting it. I am now rerunning the same goal-line harness against the warmed target/rch-ft-xbnl0-4-1-leak_risk_inventory cache.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:49:15Z"},{"id":2842,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Harness bug fixed after the latest timeout: RCH_STEP_TIMEOUT_SECS was being exported too late, after lib_rch_guards.sh had already defaulted it to 900. I moved the assignment earlier so the next run really uses a 2400-second fail-closed step timeout instead of silently inheriting 900.","created_at":"2026-04-04T02:10:17Z"},{"id":2843,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Latest fail-closed rch harness run reached core_check and exposed one additional wider-tree compile regression outside the leak-risk edits: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_session_restore.rs was still constructing RestoredPaneState without the newer scrollback_checkpoint_seq and last_output_at fields. I patched the proptest generator to include both optional fields in the generated state and rustfmt’d the test file, then prepared an immediate rerun of tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_4_1_leak_risk_inventory.sh so the evidence bundle reflects the corrected tree instead of the stale failure at artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.1/leak_risk_inventory/20260403T221022Z.","created_at":"2026-04-04T02:39:44Z"},{"id":2844,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Tracked down the latest RCH blocker to worker state, not Rust code: on vmi1227854 the remote /data/projects/frankenterm/crates tree had lost frankenterm-core, frankenterm-alloc, frankenterm-gui, frankenterm-mux-server, and frankenterm-mux-server-impl, which matches the schema_backcompat RCH-E324 dependency-preflight failure. Repaired the remote workspace non-destructively by re-syncing the local crates/ tree via rsync over SSH, verified the missing Cargo.toml files are present again, and started a fresh full harness rerun at artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.1/leak_risk_inventory/20260403T225715Z. Monitoring for whether schema_backcompat now clears remote preflight.","created_at":"2026-04-04T02:59:30Z"},{"id":2845,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"RCH worker-state blocker is resolved: schema_backcompat passed remotely on rerun 20260403T225715Z after repairing the missing remote workspace crate manifests on vmi1227854. New blocker is code-local in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_spike.rs during core_check: Select::new(...).await now yields Result<Either<...>, SelectError>, so several test assertions/matches are stale and fail to compile. Patching that test file now, then rerunning the full harness via rch.","created_at":"2026-04-04T03:20:46Z"},{"id":2846,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Diagnosed fleet-wide rch workspace truncation: vmi1153651, vmi1149989, vmi1152480, vmi1156319, vmi1167313, and vmi1293453 were missing crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs because their /data/projects/frankenterm/crates trees were truncated. Repaired non-destructively with rsync of local crates/ to each worker and verified build.rs exists on all repaired hosts. Remote clippy resumed and is now back to code-only lint debt.","created_at":"2026-04-04T04:00:25Z"},{"id":2847,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Guarded remote core_clippy on artifact 20260404T041544Z-core-clippy-guard completed fully remotely on vmi1227854 in 278420ms (exit 101, no fail-open). Current blocker moved from transport to late-stage clippy debt in tuning_config.rs, vendored/mux_client.rs, and vendored/mux_pool.rs; patching the full surfaced batch next.","created_at":"2026-04-04T04:22:32Z"},{"id":2848,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded core_clippy iteration 20260404T044245Z surfaced only two float_cmp sites in proptest_context_budget.rs before rch hit its 300s remote timeout and attempted fail-open; the guard killed the off-policy fallback as intended. Patched both comparisons to epsilon-based assertions and continuing with a targeted guarded clippy on that test before another full-crate pass.","created_at":"2026-04-04T04:50:36Z"},{"id":2849,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded targeted clippy 20260404T045048Z for proptest_context_budget completed with exit 0 on the repaired remote path after the epsilon-assertion patch, confirming the float_cmp cleanup. Re-running the full guarded frankenterm-core clippy gate now against the warmed target directory to surface the next remaining batch or clear the crate.","created_at":"2026-04-04T04:54:27Z"},{"id":2850,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T045437Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 129462ms with no fail-open. The surfaced batch collapsed to a single overly_complex_bool_expr in proptest_intervention_console.rs, so I am patching that one ordering assertion and rerunning the full guarded crate gate immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-04T04:58:58Z"},{"id":2851,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T045918Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 129042ms and surfaced only two needless_collect sites in vendored_async_contract_integration.rs. Patching those direct count assertions now and rerunning the guarded full crate gate immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:03:45Z"},{"id":2852,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T050404Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 134169ms and surfaced three redundant_pattern_matching loops in cancellation_labruntime.rs. Patching all three recv loops to use is_ok() now, then rerunning the guarded full crate gate.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:10:06Z"},{"id":2853,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"After the vmi1153651 pass timed out without surfacing lint diagnostics, I used the wasted window to proactively patch five sibling total-order proptests, one extra vendored contract split-count check, seven obvious float_cmp zero-assert sites, and the three surfaced cancellation_labruntime recv loops. No stale local cargo/clippy fallback processes remain. Relaunching guarded full core_clippy now against the warmed worker cache.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:20:34Z"},{"id":2854,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T052034Z finally broke through the long timeout churn and surfaced a highly regular vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs batch: repeated Duration::from_millis(1_000) readability lints paired with Err(_) timeout arms in the same handshake pattern. I am patching those repeated blocks directly before the next guarded rerun.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:24:57Z"},{"id":2855,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T052554Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 128237ms. The next surfaced batch is down to four items: one single-item iterator in proptest_fleet_dashboard.rs and three targeted fixes in vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs (two large_futures box-pin sites and one single_range_in_vec_init). Patching that batch now before the next guarded rerun.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:30:20Z"},{"id":2856,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T053125Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 129062ms and surfaced only two issues in swarm_simulation_regression.rs: one float_cmp on ready_ratio and one needless_collect on ready_ids. Patching both now and rerunning the guarded full crate gate.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:35:54Z"},{"id":2857,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T053614Z completed fully remote on vmi1227854 in 131966ms and collapsed to a single float_cmp in proptest_runtime_slo_gates.rs for budget_remaining == 1.0. Patching that and checking the same file for sibling float equality asserts before the next guarded rerun.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:40:44Z"},{"id":2858,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Guarded full core_clippy iteration 20260404T054105Z surfaced six identical needless_collect patterns in tx_correctness_suite.rs where thread handles are collected only to be immediately re-iterated. Patching that whole family in one sweep now before the next guarded rerun.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:45:35Z"},{"id":2859,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T05:55Z: patched tx_correctness_suite needless_collect batch and reran guarded frankenterm-core clippy via rch on vmi1227854 (artifact 20260404T054759Z-core-clippy-guard). Remote compile timed out into fail-open; guard aborted off-policy fallback before local execution. Inspecting artifact for any emitted lint before timeout.","created_at":"2026-04-04T05:55:11Z"},{"id":2860,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:00Z: reran guarded full frankenterm-core clippy on warmed vmi1227854 cache (artifact 20260404T055553Z-core-clippy-guard). This time remote exited 101 before timeout, so fail-open was avoided; harvesting the next surfaced lint batch now.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:00:07Z"},{"id":2861,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:05Z: warmed guarded core clippy artifact 20260404T060034Z surfaced next batch in proptest_robot_ntm_surface.rs: one float_cmp in roundtrip equality and two needless_collect count-only assertions. Patching all three together now.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:04:52Z"},{"id":2862,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:10Z: proactive external-test float_cmp fixes landed (docs_gen, fork_hardening_tests, quantile_sketch, usage_metrics) and guarded rerun 20260404T060616Z again exited remotely before timeout. Reading next concrete lint batch now.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:10:30Z"},{"id":2863,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:14Z: guarded full cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core --lib --tests -- -D warnings passed remotely on warmed vmi1227854 cache (artifact 20260404T061103Z-core-clippy-guard). Proceeding to full fail-closed harness tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_4_1_leak_risk_inventory.sh.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:15:28Z"},{"id":2864,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:27Z: full fail-closed harness advanced through runtime_inventory_unit, plain_surface_capture, and compact_surface_capture, then schema_roundtrip aborted because rch attempted a fail-open/off-policy path. Inspecting schema_roundtrip.log for the remote failure signature now.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:30:40Z"},{"id":2865,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T06:45Z: guarded rerun artifact 20260404T023125Z recovered cleanly from the earlier transient sync failure. runtime_inventory_unit passed in 531577ms, plain_surface_capture passed in 112328ms, compact_surface_capture passed in 111364ms; schema_roundtrip is now active remotely on vmi1227854.","created_at":"2026-04-04T06:44:42Z"},{"id":2866,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"2026-04-04T07:02Z: full fail-closed harness passed. Artifact bundle 20260404T023125Z summary=passed. schema_roundtrip/schema_backcompat recovered and passed on rerun; core_check passed in 378709ms on vmi1227854, core_clippy passed in 383540ms on vmi1227854, fmt_check passed, and source/docs inventory audits passed. Probe metadata recorded 8 reachable workers and no fail-open was detected in the passing run.","created_at":"2026-04-04T07:01:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","title":"Root-cause and fix mux-server retention and leak classes across pane tab window and session lifecycles","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the mux-server leak and retention classes that undermine the original project mission.

This task exists because the user’s motivating complaint specifically called out mux-server memory leaks. Those leaks are likely to live in lifecycle ownership, registry maps, sessionhandler state, callback or subscription cleanup, detach or reconnect behavior, and shutdown paths.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation from the preceding bead to isolate concrete mux-server leak classes.
2. Fix lifecycle ownership and cleanup bugs around pane, tab, window, domain, and session state.
3. Pay special attention to reconnect, detach, callback, and subscription cleanup semantics so old state cannot remain reachable forever.
4. Add focused regression coverage that proves the specific retention class is gone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed mux-server leak classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. Cleanup semantics for the affected lifecycle paths are explicit and test-covered.
3. Instrumentation shows the relevant counts and memory signals return to expected baselines after churn.
4. Completion evidence names the exact leak classes fixed instead of claiming generic improvement.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is the direct answer to the original mux leak complaint. It should not be allowed to close on broad hand-waving.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.587843Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.586607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","leaks","mux","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.371299Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.293064Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.526782Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.073260Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","title":"Root-cause and fix runtime, scrollback, capture, search, and file-descriptor retention outside the mux server","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the major non-mux retention and pressure sources that can still sink long-haul agent workloads.

This task exists because even after mux-server leaks are fixed, the system can still fail its mission through scrollback growth, capture backlogs, search caches, descriptor leaks, or long-lived runtime data structures that never release pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation layer to isolate pressure and retention sources outside the mux server itself.
2. Prioritize scrollback, capture, search, and descriptor lifecycle behavior because those are directly tied to multi-agent workload scale.
3. Fix ownership and reclamation bugs rather than papering over them with ad hoc trimming.
4. Add targeted regression and benchmark evidence for each important fix.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed runtime-side retention or descriptor classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. The fixes are backed by regression coverage and evidence that the relevant counters return to baseline after stress.
3. The resulting behavior is stable enough to support long-haul soak without hidden pressure growth.
4. Completion evidence distinguishes runtime-side fixes from mux-server-side fixes.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Implemented immediate runtime pane-state compaction in discovery + maintenance and search watermark pruning for disappeared panes. Added unit regressions in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/indexing_pipeline.rs, plus proof harness tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_4_3_runtime_retention.sh with artifacts under tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.3/runtime_retention/20260419T154821Z. Exact requested validation command failed on worker image because /opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang is absent (requested_worker_test.log). Worker-compatible cargo test failed in unrelated pre-existing vendored_async_contract_behavioral compile errors (worker_compatible_test.log, E0277/E0282 at lines 2182/2334).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.095566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:07:26.419069Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:07:26.418769Z","close_reason":"Implemented runtime/search retention compaction; recorded validation artifacts and unrelated worker/test blockers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd","goal-line","performance","reliability","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.627822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.023729Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.257320Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.095566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.764566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","title":"Add deterministic leak-oracle regressions for churn, reconnect, shutdown, restore, and workflow storms","description":"# Goal
Turn the leak and lifecycle fixes into deterministic regression tests that fail before future contributors reintroduce the same classes of breakage.

This task exists because long-haul issues are expensive to rediscover by soak alone. The finish-line program needs deterministic tests that model the dangerous lifecycle patterns directly.","design":"# Design
1. Build targeted churn scenarios for pane creation and teardown, reconnect, detach, shutdown, restore, and workflow-storm style activity.
2. Use deterministic runtime or focused harnesses so the results are reproducible.
3. Assert on leak-oracle signals from the instrumentation layer rather than only on functional output.
4. Keep the scenarios close enough to real workloads that later soak work benefits from the same invariants.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered lifecycle regressions have deterministic tests that fail when retention or cleanup behavior regresses.
2. The tests assert on relevant counters, ownership signals, or observable cleanup outcomes.
3. The suite is repeatable and suitable for regular remote verification.
4. Completion evidence records representative test runs and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Soak can tell us the system got sick. +{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","title":"Add deterministic leak-oracle regressions for churn, reconnect, shutdown, restore, and workflow storms","description":"# Goal
Turn the leak and lifecycle fixes into deterministic regression tests that fail before future contributors reintroduce the same classes of breakage.

This task exists because long-haul issues are expensive to rediscover by soak alone. The finish-line program needs deterministic tests that model the dangerous lifecycle patterns directly.","design":"# Design
1. Build targeted churn scenarios for pane creation and teardown, reconnect, detach, shutdown, restore, and workflow-storm style activity.
2. Use deterministic runtime or focused harnesses so the results are reproducible.
3. Assert on leak-oracle signals from the instrumentation layer rather than only on functional output.
4. Keep the scenarios close enough to real workloads that later soak work benefits from the same invariants.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered lifecycle regressions have deterministic tests that fail when retention or cleanup behavior regresses.
2. The tests assert on relevant counters, ownership signals, or observable cleanup outcomes.
3. The suite is repeatable and suitable for regular remote verification.
4. Completion evidence records representative test runs and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Soak can tell us the system got sick.  {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","title":"Build the long-haul 50, 100, and 200-pane swarm soak harness and benchmark matrix","description":"# Goal
Create the workload harness that proves FrankenTerm can actually handle the large multi-agent operating model it was built for.

This task exists because the finish line is not satisfied by unit tests and short integration runs. The project needs a representative long-haul harness that exercises the control plane, mux, scrollback, search, and operator paths under realistic swarm pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Define representative 50, 100, and 200-pane workload profiles, including idle periods, bursty output, reconnects, search, capture, and policy-sensitive actions.
2. Build a soak harness that supports both shorter developer-smoke runs and release-scale long-haul runs so the workload definitions get exercised regularly instead of only at release time.
3. Record benchmark-style metrics and artifacts during the soak so later gates can reason about trend lines rather than a single pass fail.
4. Keep the harness operator-readable, segmentable, and repeatable enough for future release cycles and incident follow-up.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A repeatable soak harness exists for representative multi-agent workloads at 50, 100, and 200 pane scale.
2. The harness records enough metrics and artifacts to diagnose failures without local guesswork.
3. The harness is tied to concrete performance and stability expectations rather than being a best-effort stress script.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative run record and artifact bundle layout.
5. The harness includes a shorter smoke profile and a release-scale long-haul profile so the same workload model is usable in both frequent validation and deep proof runs.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the bead that transforms the user’s original mission statement into an executable test campaign.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:15.827181Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","performance","reliability","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.148984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.506745Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.420840Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.729476Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.086328Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","title":"Turn runtime SLOs, soak confidence, and performance budgets into hard release gates","description":"# Goal
Convert reliability and performance evidence into explicit release gates.

This task exists because data that is not attached to a gate will be ignored the next time the schedule gets tight. The finish-line story needs hard go or no-go criteria for stability, leak behavior, and performance budgets.","design":"# Design
1. Define the SLO and budget thresholds that matter for the finish line.
2. Connect those thresholds to the instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak outputs from earlier beads.
3. Make failure modes explicit and actionable so the gates are useful rather than ceremonial.
4. Record the resulting gate definitions in bead-native form for release work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reliability and performance budgets are explicit and machine-checkable or procedure-checkable.
2. The release process has named gates for leak behavior, soak confidence, and relevant performance metrics.
3. Gate failures produce actionable diagnostics.
4. Release-readiness tasks can rely on these gates rather than reinventing them.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is what prevents multi-day swarm reliability from devolving back into a vibes-based claim.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:44:49.327598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","release-readiness","reliability","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.955060Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.766069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.385704Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5","title":"[EPIC] Release readiness, operator polish, and permanent regression guards","description":"# Goal
Turn the completed technical work into a ship-ready, operator-trustworthy project: final docs, guards, tuning guidance, end-to-end acceptance scenarios, and a closure bundle that future contributors can rely on.

This epic exists because a project can be technically improved and still not be finish-line ready if docs lie, CI does not defend the invariants, or no one has captured the evidence and workflows that define ready for real multi-agent use.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the narrative around the final native architecture and honest supported behavior.
2. Add permanent machine checks for the invariants this program establishes.
3. Expose live health and incident-diagnostics surfaces so operators can actually use the reliability work under pressure.
4. Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints so the finished system is operable from the first real interaction.
5. Validate the original mission workflows end to end, not just unit by unit.
6. Package the result into a final closure bundle with explicit remaining risks, if any.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Docs and operator guidance describe the final system rather than a migration-in-progress story.
2. CI, doctor, and audit surfaces fail fast when `tokio`, `smol`, or fake supported capabilities creep back in.
3. Operator acceptance scenarios covering the original mission are executed with artifacts.
4. A final release-candidate checklist and evidence bundle exist in bead-native form.
5. Operator-facing health and diagnostic workflows are first-class outputs of this epic, not incidental notes.
6. First-run setup and recovery entrypoints are polished enough to be part of the supported operator story rather than an undocumented initiation ritual.","notes":"This epic is what prevents the repo from sliding back into technically interesting but operationally ambiguous. It closes the loop between implementation, proof, and operator trust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:09:15.048719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","goal-line","operator","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
